Exeggcute

#0101 Electrode #0103 Exeggutor
  • Contents
  • Info
  • Base stats
  • Evolution chart
  • Pokédex entries
  • Moves learned
  • Sprites
  • Locations
  • Language

Exeggcute is a Grass/Psychic type Pokémon introduced in Generation 1.

Exeggcute

Additional artwork

Pokédex data

National № 0102
Type Grass Psychic
Species Egg Pokémon
Height 0.4 m (1′04″)
Weight 2.5 kg (5.5 lbs)
Abilities 1. Chlorophyll
Harvest (hidden ability)
Local № 0102 (Red/Blue/Yellow)
0104 (Gold/Silver/Crystal)
0102 (FireRed/LeafGreen)
0105 (HeartGold/SoulSilver)
0136 (X/Y — Coastal Kalos)
0269 (Sun/Moon — Alola dex)
0355 (U.Sun/U.Moon — Alola dex)
0102 (Let's Go Pikachu/Let's Go Eevee)
0205 (The Isle of Armor)
0003 (The Indigo Disk)

Training

EV yield 1 Defense
Catch rate 90 (11.8% with PokéBall, full HP)
Base Friendship 50 (normal)
Base Exp. 65
Growth Rate Slow

Breeding

Egg Groups Grass
Gender 50% male, 50% female
Egg cycles 20 (4,884–5,140 steps)

Base stats

HP 60
230 324
Attack 40
76 196
Defense 80
148 284
Sp. Atk 60
112 240
Sp. Def 45
85 207
Speed 40
76 196
Total 325 Min Max

The ranges shown on the right are for a level 100 Pokémon. Maximum values are based on a beneficial nature, 252 EVs, 31 IVs; minimum values are based on a hindering nature, 0 EVs, 0 IVs.

Type defenses

The effectiveness of each type on Exeggcute.

NorFirWatEleGraIceFigPoiGro
2½½½2½2½

2½422

Evolution chart

#0102
Exeggcute
Grass · Psychic
(use Leaf Stone, outside Alola)
#0103
Exeggutor
Grass · Psychic
(use Leaf Stone, in Alola)
#0103
Exeggutor
Alolan Exeggutor
Grass · Dragon

Exeggcute changes

  • In Generation 1, Exeggcute has a base Special stat of 60.
  • In Generations 1-4, Exeggcute has a base experience yield of 98.
  • In Generations 2-7, Exeggcute has a base Friendship value of 70.

Pokédex entries

Red
Blue		 Often mistaken for eggs. When disturbed, they quickly gather and attack in swarms.
Yellow The heads attract each other and spin around. There must be 6 heads for it to maintain balance.
Gold The shell is very durable. Even if it cracks, it can survive without spilling the contents.
Silver Using telepathy only they can receive, they always form a cluster of six EXEGGCUTE.
Crystal If even one is separated from the group, the energy bond between the six will make them rejoin instantly.
Ruby
Sapphire		 This POKéMON consists of six eggs that form a closely knit cluster. The six eggs attract each other and spin around. When cracks increasingly appear on the eggs, EXEGGCUTE is close to evolution.
FireRed Even though it appears to be eggs of some sort, it was discovered to be a life-form more like plant seeds.
LeafGreen It is often mistaken for eggs. When disturbed, they quickly gather and attack in swarms.
Emerald It consists of six eggs that care for each other. The eggs attract each other and spin around. When cracks increasingly appear, it is close to evolution.
Diamond
Pearl
Platinum		 Its six eggs converse using telepathy. They can quickly gather if they become separated.
HeartGold Their shells are very durable. Even if they crack, they can survive without spilling their contents.
SoulSilver Using telepathy only they can employ, they always form a cluster of six EXEGGCUTE.
Black
White
Black 2
White 2		 Its six eggs converse using telepathy. They can quickly gather if they become separated.
X Its six eggs converse using telepathy. They can quickly gather if they become separated.
Y Even though it appears to be eggs of some sort, it was discovered to be a life-form more like plant seeds.
Omega Ruby
Alpha Sapphire		 This Pokémon consists of six eggs that form a closely knit cluster. The six eggs attract each other and spin around. When cracks increasingly appear on the eggs, Exeggcute is close to evolution.
Sun Its six eggs use telepathy to communicate among themselves. It is believed to carry plant genes and the genes of other species.
Moon Six of them together form a full-fledged Pokémon. It’s often hunted by Crabrawler, but uses psychokinesis to drive it off.
Ultra Sun Six of them form a single Pokémon. Should one of the six be lost, the next morning there will once more be six.
Ultra Moon Although they are the same size as other Exeggcute, the ones produced in Alola are quite heavy. Their shells are packed full.
Let's Go Pikachu
Let's Go Eevee		 The heads attract each other and spin around. There must be six heads for it to maintain balance.
Sword Though it may look like it’s just a bunch of eggs, it’s a proper Pokémon. Exeggcute communicates with others of its kind via telepathy, apparently.
Shield These Pokémon get nervous when they’re not in a group of six. The minute even one member of the group goes missing, Exeggcute become cowardly.
Brilliant Diamond
Shining Pearl		 Its six eggs converse using telepathy. They can quickly gather if they become separated.

Legends: Z-A Scarlet/Violet

Moves learnt by level up

Exeggcute does not learn any level up moves in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

Moves learnt by TM

Exeggcute cannot be taught any TM moves in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

Moves learnt by level up

Answers to Exeggcute questions

  • If Exeggcute is put to sleep, how many of its egg's eyes close?
  • Why is Exeggcute a Psychic type?
  • In which area of the Great Marsh in Pearl can I find Exeggcute?
  • Is the egg in the back of Exeggcute dead?
  • What is a good LC moveset for Exeggcute?
  • Is Sightseer Marcus the only trainer in Pokemon Sun and Moon with a shiny or are there others?
  • Why did Exeggcute's Hypnosis not affect the enemy Rattata the first two times?

  • View more questions on PokéBase »

egg
oval object in which animals develop
execute
to exterminate
